data.name: An .apkg mixed package is Apkg's proprietary format that bundles multiple .deb packages and a manifest.xml into a single gzipped tar archive. The server unpacks each .deb, extracts metadata from its control file, and syncs them to their target APT repositories.
data.value: An .apkg mixed package is Apkg's proprietary format that bundles multiple .deb packages and a manifest.xml into a single gzipped tar archive. The server unpacks each .deb, extracts metadata from its control file, and syncs them to their target APT repositories.

data.name: and ALL its revisions? This cannot be undone.
data.value: and ALL its revisions? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: because you do not have permission to upload to them:
data.value: because you do not have permission to upload to them:

data.name: Every .apkg has three dead attributes defined at the manifest root that apply to the entire bundle: Name, Distro, and Component. These identify the package family and its target repository. They cannot vary between entries within a single .apkg.
data.value: Every .apkg has three dead attributes defined at the manifest root that apply to the entire bundle: Name, Distro, and Component. These identify the package family and its target repository. They cannot vary between entries within a single .apkg.

data.name: Latest version per (Architecture, Suite) across all uploads. These are the debs that will be sent to APT repositories.
data.value: Latest version per (Architecture, Suite) across all uploads. These are the debs that will be sent to APT repositories.

data.name: No debs in this upload. The .apkg has not been published yet.
data.value: No debs in this upload. The .apkg has not been published yet.

data.name: No packages have been uploaded yet.
data.value: No packages have been uploaded yet.

data.name: Permanently delete the entire package family
data.value: Permanently delete the entire package family

data.name: Some repositories were skipped during publish
data.value: Some repositories were skipped during publish

data.name: Use apkg push from the CLI to create and upload .apkg files. Uploads must be published before they appear in APT repositories.
data.value: Use apkg push from the CLI to create and upload .apkg files. Uploads must be published before they appear in APT repositories.

data.name: Use apkg push from the CLI to create and upload .apkg files. Uploads remain as drafts until published — only published uploads generate packages visible in APT repositories.
data.value: Use apkg push from the CLI to create and upload .apkg files. Uploads remain as drafts until published — only published uploads generate packages visible in APT repositories.

data.name: What is an APKG Mixed Package?
data.value: What is an APKG Mixed Package?

data.name: Within a single .apkg, each entry may vary on three live attributes: Version (extracted from the .deb binary, never written in manifest.xml), Suite, and Architecture. This lets you ship e.g. amd64 and arm64 builds of the same version in one upload.
data.value: Within a single .apkg, each entry may vary on three live attributes: Version (extracted from the .deb binary, never written in manifest.xml), Suite, and Architecture. This lets you ship e.g. amd64 and arm64 builds of the same version in one upload.
